narrative_for_humans.summary: Ops ran a 15% Diwali promo for Behrouz on Swiggy. Promo was supposed to end 7 days ago. Nobody flipped the flag. Discounts keep applying, net sales and payout look wrong, but the pipeline is healthy — this is an ops problem, not a data problem.
narrative_for_humans.why_this_is_interesting: Indistinguishable from a pipeline bug on the dashboard. A naive agent will blame the pipeline. A good agent will inspect the config table and realize the data is correct.

world_state.description: Hidden from the agent. Used by the environment to set up DuckDB state at reset().

injected_data_behavior.description: Last {{days_overdue}} days of orders for (brand, channel) carry the stale promo discount. All other combos behave normally.

effect.note: Discounts are numerically correct given current config — the config itself is the bug.

observation.user_complaint: Net payout for Behrouz on Swiggy looks way down this past week even though orders are steady. Discounts look bigger than expected. Is the pipeline broken or is this real?

parameterization.description: Sampled fresh each episode with a seed. Gives training variance without rewriting scenarios.

ground_truth.description: Hidden. Used only by the reward function after episode termination.

true_affected_scope.dimension_note: Anomaly is isolated to one brand-channel pair. Other combos are healthy.

required_evidence.description: Agent must query channel_brand_config and observe promo_active=true for the affected pair.
Agent should at least glance at pipeline signals to rule out a data bug. Credit for either of these.

incorrect_terminal_actions_with_reason.reason: Data is already fresh. This wastes an operation and doesn't fix anything.

trap_signals.plausible_wrong_interpretation: Agent may assume a data double-count or bad join.
trap_signals.disambiguating_check: Gross sales are consistent with order volume; only discount and net move. Raw rows match aggregate row counts.

reward_config.description: Overrides for the default reward weights for this scenario type. Null-incident scenarios reweight toward not-making-it-worse rather than fixing.

authoring_notes_for_codex.naming: scenario_id is snake_case, matches the filename. scenario_family groups related scenarios.
authoring_notes_for_codex.placeholders: Anything inside {{double_braces}} is resolved at reset() time from the parameterization block.
authoring_notes_for_codex.strict_split: Nothing in world_state, ground_truth, or authoring_notes_for_codex is ever shown to the agent. Only observation.* is shown.
authoring_notes_for_codex.null_incidents: If is_null_incident=true, correct_terminal_action must be submit_report_no_fix or similar no-op, and incorrect_terminal_actions must include any mutation.
authoring_notes_for_codex.compound_scenarios: For scenarios with multiple true causes, true_root_cause becomes a list and root_cause scoring becomes proportional. See compound_promo_plus_menu_sync.json for reference.
